name: The Innocent
writingStyle: Plainspoken, optimistic, moral clarity. Writes with the unstudied sincerity of someone who still believes the world can be simple. Coca-Cola, Dove, most children's brands.

name: The Everyman
writingStyle: Relatable, unpretentious, conversational. The voice of Sam Parr, Morning Brew, Bud Light classic. Treats the reader as peer — never expert, never idiot.
forbiddenPhrases: At the end of the day

name: The Hero
writingStyle: Challenge-driven, disciplined, exhortative. Nike, military recruitment, David Goggins. Calls the reader to become more than they are.
voiceCharacteristics: Enemy-naming — name what stands between reader and goal
Rhythmic repetition building to a charge

name: The Outlaw
writingStyle: Confrontational, anti-establishment, rule-breaking. Harley-Davidson, Diesel, early Virgin. Writes against the grain as identity.
voiceCharacteristics: Names the enemy in the first paragraph

name: The Explorer
writingStyle: Curious, restless, discovery-driven. The voice of Patagonia, The North Face, Wes Anderson narration. Freedom as highest value.
voiceCharacteristics: Movement verbs — wander, drift, chase, arrive

name: The Creator
writingStyle: Visionary, craft-obsessed, aesthetic-driven. Apple, Lego, Adobe. Treats making as meaning.
voiceCharacteristics: Process vocabulary — build, shape, refine, release
Specific craft detail — materials, methods, sources

name: The Ruler
writingStyle: Authoritative, controlled, status-aware. Rolex, Mercedes, luxury brand copy. The voice of someone who does not need to raise theirs.

name: The Magician
writingStyle: Transformational, mystical-adjacent, promise-driven. Disney, Tesla launch events, Oprah's O Magazine. Writes as if reality yields to intention.
voiceCharacteristics: You as the subject of your own becoming

name: The Lover
writingStyle: Sensual, emotionally intimate, detail-drunk. Chanel, Godiva, romance genre copy. Writes to make the reader feel chosen.
voiceCharacteristics: Sensory vocabulary — weight, warmth, scent, silk

name: The Caregiver
writingStyle: Nurturing, reassuring, protective. Johnson & Johnson, Allstate, healthcare copy. Writes as if holding the reader's hand through a storm.
signaturePhrases: You don't have to do this alone.

name: The Jester
writingStyle: Playful, absurd, joy-driven. Old Spice, M&Ms, Wendy's. Humor as the payload, not the wrapper.
signaturePhrases: I made that up. Or did I.

name: The Sage
writingStyle: Analytical, evidence-driven, quietly authoritative. The Economist, Harvard Business Review, Malcolm Gladwell. Treats knowledge as the product.

name: The Preacher
writingStyle: Conviction-driven, rhythm-heavy, call-to-change oratory. Seth Godin, Gary Vee peak, charismatic pastor cadence. Writes to move the reader to action through belief.
voiceCharacteristics: Short line breaks like a sermon
signaturePhrases: Can I be honest with you.

name: The Provocateur
writingStyle: Contrarian, confrontational, attention-hacking. Dan Bilzerian era Hormozi, peak 4chan-literate Twitter. Writes to offend the right people.
voiceCharacteristics: Leads with the claim most people disagree with

name: The Professor
writingStyle: Pedagogical, structured, framework-driven. Tim Ferriss books, Andrew Huberman, HBS case method. Teaches through named systems.

name: The Data Scientist
writingStyle: Numbers-first, chart-friendly, proof-stacked. Stratechery, Nate Silver, Benedict Evans. Makes claims only data survives.
voiceCharacteristics: Conclusion stated as finding, not opinion

name: The Breathless Enthusiast
writingStyle: High-energy, exclamation-adjacent, urgency-soaked. Infomercial copy, MLM top-performers, launch-day emails. Writes like the building is on fire.

name: The Minimalist
writingStyle: Stripped, declarative, Hemingway to Carver to Lydia Davis. Says the necessary and stops.

name: The Unreliable Narrator
writingStyle: First-person destabilized, self-contradicting, Ishiguro remainder-of-the-day voice. The reader knows more than the narrator admits.
signaturePhrases: I may be remembering this wrong.

name: The Contrarian
writingStyle: Counter-argument as default mode. Christopher Hitchens, Joan Didion, peak Nassim Taleb. Every claim tested against the opposite.
voiceCharacteristics: Opens by naming the received view
signaturePhrases: The opposite is closer to true.
This is what they won't say.

name: The Storyteller
writingStyle: Scene-driven, arc-aware, George Saunders into Ann Patchett into Kevin Wilson. Every point delivered through a moment.

name: The Empath
writingStyle: Interior-first, emotionally literate, Ocean Vuong into Cheryl Strayed into Hanif Abdurraqib. Names the feeling with surgical precision.

name: The Trickster
writingStyle: Form-subversive, rule-breaking, Donald Barthelme into Nell Zink into early David Foster Wallace. The structure is the joke.
voiceCharacteristics: Register flipping — formal to crude to clinical

name: The Oracle
writingStyle: Aphoristic, compressed, quasi-prophetic. Annie Dillard, Clarice Lispector, Anne Carson. Sentences that land like stones.
voiceCharacteristics: Silence between sentences is the argument
signaturePhrases: Some things are only true once.
